- apex: Systematic implementation using APEX methodology (Analyze-Plan-Execute-Validate) with parallel agents and self-validation. Use when implementing features, fixing bugs, or making code changes that benefit from structured workflow.
- claude-memory: Create and optimize CLAUDE.md memory files or .claude/rules/ modular rules for Claude Code projects. Comprehensive guidance on file hierarchy, content structure, path-scoped rules, best practices, and anti-patterns. Use when working with CLAUDE.md files, .claude/rules directories, setting up new projects, or improving Claude Code's context awareness.
- codex-environment: Create, inspect, or repair Codex app Local Environment configuration for worktrees, including .codex/environments/environment.toml, setup and cleanup scripts, worktree up/down scripts, copied env files, isolated databases, and reusable app actions.
- commit: Quick commit and push with minimal, clean messages
- create-pr: Create and push PR with auto-generated title and description
- fix-errors: Fix all ESLint and TypeScript errors with parallel processing using snipper agents
- fix-grammar: Fix grammar and spelling errors in one or multiple files while preserving formatting
- fix-pr-comments: Fetch PR review comments and implement all requested changes
- merge: Intelligently merge branches with context-aware conflict resolution
- oneshot: Ultra-fast feature implementation using Explore → Code → Test workflow. Use when implementing focused features, single tasks, or when speed over completeness is priority.
- prompt-creator: Expert prompt engineering for creating effective prompts for Claude, GPT, and other LLMs. Use when writing system prompts, user prompts, few-shot examples, or optimizing existing prompts for better performance.
- ralph-loop: Setup the Ralph autonomous AI coding loop - ships features while you sleep
- subagent-creator: Expert guidance for creating, building, and using Claude Code subagents and the Task tool. Use when working with subagents, setting up agent configurations, understanding how agents work, or using the Task tool to launch specialized agents.
- ultrathink: Deep thinking mode - approach problems like a craftsman, obsess over details, and create elegant solutions
- workflow-apex-free: Systematic implementation using APEX methodology (Analyze-Plan-Execute-Validate) with parallel agents and self-validation. Use when implementing features, fixing bugs, or making code changes that benefit from structured workflow.
- frontend-design: Create distinctive, production-grade frontend interfaces with high design quality. Use this skill when the user asks to build web components, pages, artifacts, posters, or applications (examples include websites, landing pages, dashboards, React components, HTML/CSS layouts, or when styling/beautifying any web UI). Generates creative, polished code and UI design that avoids generic AI aesthetics.
- find-skills: Helps users discover and install agent skills when they ask questions like "how do I do X", "find a skill for X", "is there a skill that can...", or express interest in extending capabilities. This skill should be used when the user is looking for functionality that might exist as an installable skill.
- skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- context7-mcp: This skill should be used when the user asks about libraries, frameworks, API references, or needs code examples. Activates for setup questions, code generation involving libraries, or mentions of specific frameworks like React, Vue, Next.js, Prisma, Supabase, etc.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text: I'll read the file, fix French grammar/spelling errors, then edit it.

Base directory for this skill: /home/Maxime/.claude/skills/fix-grammar

# Fix Grammar

Fix grammar and spelling errors in files while preserving formatting and meaning.

## Workflow

1. **PARSE FILES**: Split arguments into individual file paths
   - **STOP** if no files specified - ask user for file paths

2. **DETERMINE STRATEGY**:
   - **Single file**: Process directly
   - **Multiple files**: Launch parallel fix-grammar agents

3. **SINGLE FILE MODE**:
   - `Read` the file completely
   - Apply grammar and spelling corrections
   - `Edit` to update file with corrections

4. **MULTIPLE FILES MODE**:
   - Use Task tool to launch fix-grammar agent for each file
   - Process all files simultaneously
   - Wait for all agents to complete

5. **REPORT**: Show files processed and confirm corrections

## Correction Rules

- Fix ONLY spelling and grammar errors
- **DO NOT** change meaning or word order
- **DO NOT** translate anything
- **DO NOT** modify special tags (MDX, custom syntax, code blocks)
- **PRESERVE**: All formatting, structure, technical terms
- Remove any `"""` markers if present
- Keep the same language used in each sentence
- Handle multilingual content (keep anglicisms, technical terms)

## Output Format

```
✓ Fixed grammar in [filename]
- [number] corrections made
```

## Rules

- ONLY spelling and grammar corrections
- PARALLEL processing for multiple files
- PRESERVE everything: formatting, structure, technical terms
- MINIMAL changes - corrections only, no improvements
- Never add explanations or commentary to file content

content.content:
1 # Design System — DNGBlog 2 3 Ne jamais modifier le design existant sans demande explicite. Respecter strictement les conventions ci-dessous. 4 5 --- 6 7 ## Identité visuelle 8 9 **Style** : Minimaliste tech — angles droits, bordures pointillées, ombres 3D multidirectionnelles. 10 **Police** : Roboto (définie via `@theme static` dans `main.css`) 11 **Border-radius** : Toujours `rounded-none` — aucune exception. 12 13 --- 14 15 ## Couleurs 16 17 ### Variables CSS personnalisées (définies dans `main.css`) 18 ``` 19 --color-CustomColor-900: rgb(10, 10, 10) // fond dark mode 20 --color-CustomLight: #FFF // fond light mode 21 --color-dashcolor: #E5E7EB44 // bordures pointillées dark 22 ``` 23 24 ### Palette exacte 25 | Usage | Light | Dark | 26 |-------|-------|------| 27 | Fond principal | `#FFF` (CustomLight) | `#0A0A0A` (CustomColor-900) | 28 | Fond alternatif | `#EEE` | `#111` | 29 | Texte principal | `#0F0F0F` | `#FFFFFF` | 30 | Texte secondaire | `#0F0F0F/70` | `#F3F4F6/70` | 31 | Texte tertiaire | `zinc-500` | `zinc-500` | 32 | Erreurs | `red-400` / `red-500` | idem | 33 | Accent bleu | `blue-500` | idem | 34 35 ### Couleurs catégories (OKLCH) 36 ``` 37 Vue.js : oklch(62.7% 0.194 149.214) 38 Tailwind : oklch(54.6% 0.245 262.881) 39 CSS : oklch(70.7% 0.165 254.624) 40 ``` 41 42 ### Config (`app.config.ts`) 43 - Primary : `green` 44 - Secondary : `blue` 45 - Neutral : `CustomColor` 46 47 --- 48 49 ## Ombres (pattern répété partout) 50 51 ``` 52 shadow-[6px_-7px_24px_0px_rgb(0,0,0,0.51)] 53 shadow-[-6px_7px_24px_0px_rgb(0,0,0,0.51)] 54 shadow-[0px_-4px_4px_0px_rgb(0,0,0,0.51)] 55 ``` 56 Crée un effet 3D avec ombres multidirectionnelles. Utilisé sur : cartes, inputs, boutons, formulaires. 57 58 Ombre nav au scroll : `rgba(0,0,0,0.16) 0px 3px 6px, rgba(0,0,0,0.23) 0px 3px 6px` 59 60 --- 61 62 ## Bordures 63 64 - Épaisseur : `border-[0.1px]` (très fine) 65 - Style : `border-dashed` (sections, header par défaut), `border-solid` (header au scroll) 66 - Couleur light : `border-CustomColor-900/60`, `border-primary/30` 67 - Couleur dark : `border-dashcolor`, `border-dashcolor/50` 68 69 --- 70 71 ## Pattern de carte / conteneur (réutiliser tel quel) 72 73 ```html 74 class="bg-CustomLight dark:bg-CustomColor-900 75 border-[0.1px] border-primary/30 dark:border-dashcolor/50 76 shadow-[6px_-7px_24px_0px_rgb(0,0,0,0.51)] 77 shadow-[-6px_7px_24px_0px_rgb(0,0,0,0.51)] 78 shadow-[0px_-4px_4px_0px_rgb(0,0,0,0.51)] 79 rounded-none" 80 ``` 81 Utilisé dans : `ArticleCard`, `LoginForm`, `RegisterForm`, `ForgotForm`, `ResetForm`, `CUButton`, `CUInput`, `NewsLetter`. 82 83 --- 84 85 ## Composants custom (préfixe CU) 86 87 ### `CUButton` (wrapper UButton) 88 ``` 89 rounded-none 90 border-[0.1px] border-dashcolor/50 91 shadow triple (voir ci-dessus) 92 hover:bg-primary 93 ``` 94 95 ### `CUInput` (wrapper UInput) 96 ``` 97 rounded-none 98 bg-CustomLight dark:bg-CustomColor-900 99 border-CustomColor-900/30 dark:border-dashcolor/50 100 shadow-[-6px_7px_24px_0px_rgb(0,0,0,0.51)] shadow-[0px_-4px_4px_0px_rgb(0,0,0,0.51)] 101 placeholder-[#0F0F0F]/40 dark:placeholder-[#F3F4F6]/60 102 ``` 103 104 **Toujours utiliser `CUButton` et `CUInput` à la place de `UButton` et `UInput` directement.** 105 106 --- 107 108 ## Layout de page (structure standard) 109 110 ```html 111 112 113 114 115 116 ``` 117 118 Pour les pages de formulaire (auth) : 119 ```html 120 121 122
123 124
125 126 127 ``` 128 129 --- 130 131 ## Typographie 132 133 | Élément | Classes | 134 |---------|---------| 135 | Hero H1 | `text-[24px] sm:text-[32px] lg:text-[40px] font-bold` | 136 | Header H1 | `text-xl lg:text-2xl font-semibold` | 137 | Titre section | `text-[24px] sm:text-[32px] lg:text-[38px] font-bold` | 138 | H2 formulaire | `text-3xl font-bold` | 139 | H3 carte | `text-2xl font-semibold` | 140 141 --- 142 143 ## Espacements 144 145 - Container global : `max-w-7xl` 146 - Padding horizontal : `px-4` (mobile), `px-8` (desktop) 147 - Cartes : `p-6` ou `p-8` 148 - Sections : `pt-20 mb-10 px-4` 149 - Champs formulaire : `mb-6` 150 151 --- 152 153 ## Backgrounds spéciaux 154 155 ### `.bgHero` (Hero section — défini dans `main.css`) 156 - Light : `#EEE` avec points `radial-gradient(circle, #888 2px, transparent 1px)` 22x22px + noise SVG + fade elliptique 157 - Dark : `#111` avec points `#555` + fade elliptique vers `#111` 158 159 ### `.bgCategorie` 160 - Light : `#DEDEDE` avec même structure que bgHero 161 162 **Ne pas recréer ces classes inline — toujours utiliser les classes CSS définies.** 163 164 --- 165 166 ## Animations 167 168 ### Carousels catégories (CSS pur, `main.css`) 169 - `scrolling` : de gauche à droite, 20s linear infinite 170 - `scrollingRight` : de droite à gauche, 20s linear infinite 171 - Pause au hover : `animation-play-state: paused` 172 173 ### Transitions 174 - Header au scroll : `transition-all duration-300` + `backdrop-blur-sm` 175 - Hover cartes : `transition hover:border-blue-500/50` 176 - Durée standard : 300ms 177 178 --- 179 180 ## Dark mode 181 182 - Basé sur `colorMode` de Nuxt 183 - Toujours doubler les classes : `bg-CustomLight dark:bg-CustomColor-900` 184 - Le header, footer, et toutes les sections doivent supporter les deux modes 185 186 --- 187 188 ## Header 189 190 - Fixe (`fixed top-0`), `z-50` 191 - Hauteur : `h-12 lg:h-16` 192 - Fond : `bg-CustomLight dark:bg-CustomColor-900` 193 - Border bottom dashed par défaut → solid + shadow + `backdrop-blur-sm` au scroll (y >= 300px) 194 - Max-width interne : `max-w-7xl` 195 196 --- 197 198 ## Modales 199 200 ``` 201 overlay: 'bg-CustomColor-900/60 dark:bg-CustomColor-900/60 backdrop-blur-xs' 202 content: 'ring-0 flex justify-center bg-transparent items-center' 203 ``` 204 205 --- 206 207 ## Règles absolues 208 209 1. `rounded-none` partout — jamais de border-radius 210 2. Toujours les 3 ombres directionnelles sur les éléments en relief 211 3. `border-dashed border-[0.1px]` pour les délimitations de sections 212 4. Toujours `CUButton` et `CUInput`, jamais `UButton`/`UInput` directement 213 5. Responsive mobile-first : défaut → `sm:` → `md:` → `lg:` 214 6. Pas de couleurs hardcodées autres que celles de la palette — utiliser les variables CSS 215
